meaning: n.婴儿,被精心培育的东西
examples: On the morning of a fine June day my first Bonny little nursling, the last of the ancient Earnshaw stock, was born.
On the morning of a fine June day, my first Bonny little nursling, and the last of the ancient Earnshaw stock, was born.
For nursling of plantlets, the soil of the rotten leaves was the best materials, and the survival rate could be up to 80%.
In 1913, when Frank was substituting for Taylor in Chicago, Lillian went along, with a three-month-old nursling.
